# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD Pipeline for GitHub Actions Workflows 
on: [pull_request] # Triggered when a pull request is created or updated, based on specific event types like 'create' and 'push'. You can customize this to run on other events as well. Required whenever you want the action triggered from git hub actions workflow triggers list of options are there in github repo settings under Actions
jobs:  # These represent individual jobs that a multi-job runner manages, where each job runs sequentially inside its own instance based environment and does not cross instances for concurrent execution. Required to name the section uniquely which can be used by subsequent steps/actions in your workflow or across different machines with running this command on machine A
  build_notifications:   # This is a single step where you run actions that produce output, use outputs from previous jobs and finally publish results as artifacts. When we talk about "artifact" here it means the code which will be sent back to repository for further execution in next steps/actions downstream or even on different machines with running this command
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest # The type of machine that should have these actions executed against (required and can also accept a matrix). Required if you want your job run after the specific configuration changes across all items, only when those are explicitly declared. You could replace "ubuntu" to other CI/CD platforms e.g., windows latest for Windows based scenarios
    steps: # These represent individual jobs that an actions runner manages inside its own instance and do not cross instances for concurrent execution like a single step function in your workflow, where you run all the necessary commands or tasks required when using this job to execute git command/actions on specific configurations. Required if there are complex scenarios
      - name: Checkout code  # This action will check out any dependencies found under .github directory (this is done for 'checkouts' in case of private repos) and commit details which can be accessed through $GITHUB_SHA,$GITHUB_REPOSITORY. Required to ensure that the actions are happening inside your GitHub repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v2  # This action will clone repo using provided token (for private repositories) and set current working directory which can be accessed later on as `workspace` or if not defined then it defaults into root of project. Required for the git command being done inside this job
      - name: Create Release Note   # Action to create release notes, use GitHub's in-built feature where we will capture title and description from pr details while creating notification via Github Actions with specific requirements mentioned above (PR Title + PR Description). Required when there are complex scenarios. 
        id: creationOfReleaseNotes    # Unique ID for this job, required to reference later downstream actions/utils#create-release action requires a particular unique string as input which is created by the user on Github UI or via YAML file if specified in 'with' field inside step. Required
        uses: peter-evans/create-or-update-release@v1  # This GitHub Action will create release notes using title and description provided to this action as input, with a tag from the PR details when creating it through actions or utilities where user can specify specific requirements. Required
        env:    # Define environment variables for your job/actions run inside (required if you want variable values in different instances of same type). It provides access point using ${{ secrets.[name_of__secret] }}.  You're setting up a secret which holds sensitive data and is accessible within this action. Required when there are complex scenarios
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}   # This acts as personal access token to authenticate actions with GitHub, required for all of the following tasks in 'with' field inside step which runs on git command/actions is happening within this job and requires a context that doesn’t contain sensitive data or can be accessed by users who have write permissions. 
          RELEASE_NAME: ${{ steps.creationOfReleaseNotes.outputs.release-name }}   # This will capture the release name which was created during 'step' execution within this job and use it for further downstream actions/utils#create-or-update--release action as input, with title set from PR details when creating new note via Github Actions or utilities where user can specify specific requirements. Required
          RELEASE_BODY: |    # This is used to capture the body of release notes which was captured by 'step' execution inside this job and use it for further downstream actions/utils#create-or-update--release action as input, with title set from PR details when creating new note via Github Actions or utilities where user can specify specific requirements. 
            Title: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}   # This is used to capture the pr number which was captured by 'github' variable inside this job and use it for further downstream actions/utils#create-or-update--release action as input, with title set from PR details when creating new note via Github Actions or utilities where user can specify specific requirements. Required
            Body: ${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}   # This is used to capture the pr description which was captured by 'github' variable inside this job and use it for further downstream actions/utils#create-or-update--release action as input, with title set from PR details when creating new note via Github Actions or utilities where user can specify specific requirements. Required
